Title: CESA7 and microtubules pattern complex secondary cell walls in explosive fruit of Cardamine hirsuta

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Atypical tubulin kinase truncation (microtubule depolymerizing construct)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: secondary cell wall cellulose; lignin; xylan
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary wall architecture enabling explosive seed dispersal
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit endocarp b secondary cell wall; root metaxylem secondary cell wall
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in fruit endocarp b cells after dexamethasone treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 1 mM dexamethasone on agar or 2 mM dexamethasone dipping
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced microtubule depolymerization abolished hinged secondary cell wall patterning and prevented explosive valve coiling
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dex-inducible expression; GFP-TUA6 imaging of microtubule loss; histological staining of cellulose/lignin/xylan; fruit coiling assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Nonexplosive fruits; valves fail to coil
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an experimental tool to test wall-patterning mechanisms, not a direct breeding target
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Induced PHS1ΔP expression fully depolymerized microtubules in endb cells. 2. Loss of microtubules eliminated hinged SCW domains and caused thickened unpatterned walls. 3. Fruits failed to explosively coil, showing microtubule-dependent SCW patterning is functionally essential.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a perturbation construct rather than a native nutrient-metabolism gene.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Secondary cell wall cellulose synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes glucose from UDP-glucose into cellulose microfibrils in the secondary cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural scaffold for ordered secondary wall assembly and explosive seed dispersal mechanics
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit endocarp b secondary cell wall; root metaxylem secondary cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endb cells before and during SCW formation and in other SCW-forming cells such as protoxylem/root xylem
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in endb cells before and during SCW formation and in protoxylem/root xylem
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: microtubules; secondary wall cellulose synthase complex components
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CRISPR loss-of-function; oryzalin co-treatment; cellulose inhibitor treatments tested but ineffective for SCW in study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for SCW cellulose accumulation; loss causes severe cellulose depletion, abnormal SCW overgrowth/pattern maintenance defects, reduced valve coiling, dwarfism, shorter fruits, and irregular metaxylem pits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout alleles; Arabidopsis irx3-4 mutant analysis; cross-species complementation with pCESA7::mNG:CESA7; reporter expression; cellulose quantification; microscopy; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A major target for engineering secondary wall architecture and dispersal/mechanical traits, but loss of function causes dwarfism and reduced fruit length so complete knockout is undesirable
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarf plants; shorter fruits; collapsed xylem vessels; reduced explosive coiling
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase A family, membrane glycosyltransferase with multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA7 is essential for secondary wall cellulose biosynthesis in endocarp b cells of Cardamine hirsuta and Arabidopsis. 2. In cesa7 mutants, lignin and xylan are still deposited in an initial hinged pattern, but mature wall geometry and layered architecture cannot be maintained. 3. CESA7-dependent cellulose acts as a scaffold for ordered assembly of SCW polymers and for proper explosive fruit coiling. 4. CESA7 also maintains regular pit patterning in root metaxylem SCWs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In cesa7-1 fruit valves only 46.9% of wild-type cellulose remained; lignin and xylan increased proportionally. Only CESA7 among expressed CESA genes was differentially expressed in mutant valves. Function was highly conserved between Cardamine and Arabidopsis.
